issue_title: ROCm, 7900 XTX (gfx1100): Pytorch FLASH_ATTENTION SDPA is 2.5x slower than MATH (fp16, head_dim 256, seqlen 4360, 12 heads)
surface_reasoning: Priority 2: labeled `module: performance` + `module: rocm` + `module: sdpa`. Title: 'ROCm FLASH_ATTENTION SDPA is 2.5x slower than MATH (fp16, head_dim 256, seqlen 4360)' — concrete 2.5x regression where the specialized flash attention kernel underperforms the naive math backend. Domain relevance: ROCm flash attention is a fused HIP kernel; performing worse than the math fallback indicates a kernel configuration or dispatch bug in the ROCm SDPA implementation.

maintainer_signals.author: naromero77amd
maintainer_signals.quote: Could you please post the final updated version of your benchmark? Thanks

maintainer_signals.author: xinyazhang
maintainer_signals.quote: The performance depends on the Triton/LLVM optimization on Navi31

maintainer_signals.author: naromero77amd
maintainer_signals.quote: No, there is no other branch to try. What @xinyazhang meant is that performance issues are dependent on Triton compiler toolchain

blocking_signals: fix_requires_external_lib: Performance of ROCm SDPA flash attention on Navi31 (gfx1100) depends on Triton/LLVM compiler optimization; maintainer xinyazhang confirmed fix requires improvements in the external Triton toolchain, not PyTorch itself
confidence_justification: Step 1: Start at 0.50. Step 2: 'triaged' label present (+0.10). Running total: 0.60. Step 3: Repro script present — fully self-contained benchmark is in the issue body (+0.15). Running total: 0.75. Step 4: fix_requires_external_lib in blocking_signals — COLLABORATOR xinyazhang explicitly stated 'The performance depends on the Triton/LLVM optimization on Navi31' and naromero77amd confirmed 'performance issues are dependent on Triton compiler toolchain'; fix requires improvements in the external Triton/LLVM toolchain, not PyTorch itself (-0.30). Running total: 0.45. Step 5: No 'module: correctness (silent)' label — this is a performance issue only; no +0.25 correctness signal. Step 6: still_reproducible='confirmed' — user confirmed issue still present on ROCm 7 as recently as late 2025 (within 12 months); no reproducibility penalty. Step 7: Issue is ~1 year old as of 2026-04-24 — not yet 2+ years, so no +0.05 persistence bonus. Step 8: fix_scope is requires_external_lib — no +0.10 single_function/single_file bonus. Final: clamp(0.45, 0.0, 1.0) = 0.45.
ground_truth_delta: Similar to ambiguous_72408 (integer matmul 30x slower): both are real, reproducible performance issues that require external toolchain or library improvements rather than a PyTorch code change. Key differences: (1) 152595 is a ROCm/Triton dependency rather than integer BLAS; (2) no explicit maintainer 'no real way to act' comment was made — the maintainers are actively investigating but scoped the root cause to Triton/LLVM; (3) issue is more recent (~1 year vs 4+ years); (4) maintainers have not explicitly blocked action, only identified that improvement depends on external Triton optimization. Slightly more actionable than 72408 since no explicit blocked_action signal exists, but still unlikely to result in a PyTorch-only fix PR.
